articles.description: Debutant seamer Matthew Hollard was named Player of the Match for his 3/28 figures in Ireland's historic first-ever international win against India. The hosts defended 183, restricting India to 148 to take a 1-0 lead in the T20I series.

articles.title: What is Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller’s theory on Auston Matthews production dip?
articles.description: New Toronto Maple Leafs head coach Jim Hiller believes Auston Matthews' recent goal production dip isn't due to individual decline, but rather the team's struggling power play. Hiller, who previously coached Matthews as an assistant, aims to revitalize the special teams, hoping it will positively impact the star center's five-on-five performance. Matthews is also recovering from injuries and is expected to return soon.

articles.title: John Abraham joins Rotterdam Dockers as co-owner ahead of European T20 Premier League debut
articles.description: John Abraham has acquired a stake in the Rotterdam Dockers, a franchise for the upcoming European T20 Premier League. Set to launch in August 2026, the league aims to foster European cricket talent and will feature six city-based teams. Abraham joins a distinguished ownership group including cricketing legends, underscoring the league's ambition to elevate the sport across the continent.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | 'Spain have the edge': Ashley Westwood backs La Roja ahead of Uruguay clash
articles.description: Spain vs Uruguay: Ashley Westwood backed Spain to beat Uruguay in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H clash, praising Lamine Yamal's brilliance and saying La Roja's midfield and possession game give them the advantage.

articles.title: Under Gambhir's watchful eye, Sooryavanshi unleashes fiery shots - Watch
articles.description: Fifteen-year-old batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has trained with the senior Indian cricket team ahead of their T20I series against Ireland. Spotted practicing attacking shots under coach Gautam Gambhir, Sooryavanshi could become India's youngest international debutant, potentially breaking Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record. His inclusion presents a selection dilemma for the team management, balancing a winning combination with the excitement surrounding the young star.

articles.title: NRAI's Shotgun Referee Course aims to boost officiating standards
articles.description: The NRAI has launched a four-day Shotgun Referee Course for 14 participants to enhance officiating standards. The initiative aims to build a world-class pool of certified officials, ensuring Indian domestic competitions align with global standards.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 creates history as highest-scoring, most-attended tournament
articles.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 etched its name into the record books on Friday after becoming the highest-scoring edition in the tournament's history, with the landmark being reached during the United States' thrilling Group D clash against Turkey.

articles.title: More than 100 women players sign up for ICC's protection programme against online trolling
articles.description: Over 100 women cricketers are now protected from online abuse through the ICC Player Protection Programme, powered by AI. During the T20 World Cup, nearly 60,000 harmful comments were removed, with repeat offenders facing restrictions. Players like Radha Yadav and Amy Jones highlight the program's importance in combating social media toxicity and safeguarding athlete well-being, allowing them to engage with fans more confidently.

articles.title: Aaditya Thackeray Hits Out at BJP Over Mumbai Football Ground Row
articles.description: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Aaditya Thackeray criticised the BJP for converting Neville D'Souza football ground in Bandra into a convention centre, alleging it deprives youth of playgrounds to benefit contractors and worsen traffic.

articles.title: Future Star U-15: Meerut Spartans beat Noida Blaze in rain-hit game

articles.title: “She should be banned indefinitely”: Furious Caitlin Clark fans slam WNBA’s soft punishment to Alyssa Thomas for hitting Fever star’s throat
articles.description: The WNBA suspended Phoenix Mercury forward Alyssa Thomas for one game after retroactively upgrading her contact with Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark to a Flagrant Foul 2. The incident, which officials missed during the game, drew widespread criticism from fans who argued the punishment was too light. Fever coach Stephanie White strongly condemned the missed call, while team president Kelly Krauskopf emphasized that player safety must remain the league's top priority as Indiana prepares for its next game.
